Days 1–4

Paris

Four days in the first arrondissement: an overnight arrival, the Louvre, the Eiffel Tower at opening, a Versailles day trip, and golden hour over Montmartre.

Home base

Paris, 1st Arrondissement

Jul 4 – Jul 8Elixir Hotel
Open our saved Paris map →
Day 1 · 9:35 PM

Depart Newark on United UA 54

Dinner is served onboard; settle in for the overnight crossing to Paris. Kickoff back home: Canada vs Morocco and Paraguay vs France both play before your evening boarding.

Day 2 · 2:00 PM

Louvre Museum — Matusov family

Land at CDG 10:55 AM, drop bags at the hotel, then the Matusov family takes the afternoon Louvre slot. Pace a focused route toward the signature masterpieces. O'Brien family: free afternoon nearby, with the Tuileries Garden and Rue de Rivoli both a short walk.

✦Nearby sights
  • Jardin des Tuileries formal gardens between Louvre and Place de la Concorde
  • Place Vendôme octagonal square with the Ritz and Napoleon's column
  • Palais Royal Colonnaded courtyard and striped Buren columns
Plan your visit
◉Eat & drink
  • Stohrer Paris's oldest patisserie (1730), rue Montorgueil
  • Rue Montorgueil pedestrian street of cafes near the hotel
⚽World Cup watch

First full day in Paris. It's confirmed: Brazil face Norway tonight, around 10 PM Paris time — Norway edged out Ivory Coast 2–1 to set up the tie. Morning highlights work just as well.

Day 2 · 5:30 PM

Notre-Dame Cathedral

Since reopening in December 2024 after the fire restoration, the cathedral's interior glows with freshly cleaned stone and stained glass. Walk the plaza out front for the full facade view, then cross to the quiet Île Saint-Louis promenade for ice cream at Berthillon before dinner.

Plan your visit
Day 3 · 9:30 AM

Eiffel Tower summit, then the Champs

Morning ascent for early daylight across the city; book ahead to keep the line short with two families. Afternoon stroll down the Champs-Elysees to the Arc de Triomphe, with Sainte-Chapelle later on.

✦Along the way
  • Arc de Triomphe climb the roof for the best view down the Champs
  • Sainte-Chapelle 13th-century stained-glass masterpiece on Île de la Cité
  • Conciergerie former royal palace and revolutionary prison, next door to Sainte-Chapelle
Book tickets
◉Eat & drink
  • Dumbo Smash Burgers quick, kid-friendly stop
  • Homer Lobster casual New-England lobster rolls
⚽World Cup watch

Round of 16 doubleheader today. / Portugal or Croatia meet / Spain or Austria — those two ties are actually being played today (July 2), so the matchup locks in before you land. USA also face Belgium the same day. The evening match plays around 9 PM Paris time, easy to catch after dinner.

Day 4 · Morning

Versailles, then Montmartre at sunset

Ride the RER C straight to Versailles Chateau Rive Gauche; head first for the Hall of Mirrors before midday crowds build. Back in the city, wind through Montmartre's artist lanes and gather on the Sacre-Coeur steps for golden hour.

Plan your visit
◉Eat & drink
  • Le Consulat 1800s artists' bistro on Rue Norvins
  • La Maison Rose the iconic pink cafe; go early
  • Place du Tertre artist square with portrait painters open late
  • Sacré-Coeur steps grab a crepe and watch the sunset over the city
⚽World Cup watch

Round of 16 closes out: / Argentina or Cape Verde meet the / Australia/Egypt winner, and / Switzerland or Algeria face the / Colombia/Ghana winner — those pairings depend on July 3 results. Early match around 6 PM Paris time, second around 10 PM.

Day 4 · 9:00 PM

Seine River Evening Cruise

Cap off the day with a Bateaux Parisiens sightseeing cruise from Port de la Bourdonnais, right at the foot of the Eiffel Tower. The hour-long loop passes Notre-Dame, the Louvre, and Musée d'Orsay, timed after dark to catch the Eiffel Tower's hourly sparkle from the water.

Book the cruise
Days 5–6

Mykonos

Iconic whitewashed Cycladic beauty: Little Venice at golden hour, the famous windmills, and the gateway to the islands by Seajets ferry.

The Cyclades

Mykonos Town

Jul 8 – Jul 9Overnight in Mykonos Town
Open in Google Maps
Day 5 · Evening

Arrive Mykonos

Air France AF 1098 lands at Mykonos (JMK) at 6:20 PM. Explore Little Venice and walk through the iconic whitewashed streets of Mykonos Town. Grab dinner with a sea view and watch the sunset over the windmills.

Open in Google Maps
◉Eat & drink
  • Kostas Taverna traditional Greek waterfront, Mykonos Town
  • Scorpios Mykonos sunset cocktails on Paraga Beach (taxi ride)
⚽World Cup watch

A rest night for the tournament: the Round of 16 wrapped July 7 and quarterfinals do not begin until July 9.

Day 6 · Morning

Ferry Mykonos → Naxos

Morning ferry from Mykonos to Naxos (about 40 minutes). Settle into Aeolis Boutique Hotel and grab lunch in Naxos Town before exploring.

Days 6–7

Naxos

The greenest Cyclade: the marble Portara at sunset, an inland road trip through ancient temples and mountain villages, and long golden beaches for the kids.

The Cyclades

Naxos Town & Beyond

Jul 9 – Jul 11Aeolis Boutique Hotel
Open in Google Maps
Day 6 · Sunset

The Portara, Temple of Apollo

Naxos's icon: a giant 6th-century BC marble doorway on the Palatia islet, reached by a short causeway from Chora. It faces Delos and frames the island's best sunset.

Open in Google Maps
Day 6 · Road trip

Temple of Demeter & the mountain villages

Rent a car or ATV for the interior: the marble Temple of Demeter near Sangri, the marble-paved village of Apeiranthos, and the Kitron liqueur distillery at Halki.

Day 7 · Beach day

Plaka & Agios Prokopios

Endless golden sand at Plaka; calm, crystal-clear water at Agios Prokopios. Both are an easy hop from Chora and ideal for the kids.

◉Eat & drink
  • Taverna Naxos waterfront family spot, sunset over the Portara
  • Axiotissa beloved farm-to-table taverna in the south
⚽World Cup watch

Quarterfinal 1 (Thu Jul 9) around 11 PM Athens time; Quarterfinal 2 (Fri Jul 10) around 10 PM. Ask your taverna if they will have it on.

Days 8–10

Paros

Beaches by day, harbor by night: the sculpted coves of Kolymbithres, the Byzantine Church of 100 Doors in Parikia, and Naoussa's candlelit waterfront after dark.

The Cyclades

Naoussa & Parikia

Jul 11 – Jul 14Play Paros
Open in Google Maps
Day 8 · Daytime

Kolymbithres & Santa Maria

Swim among Kolymbithres' sculpted granite coves, or spend a social beach day at Santa Maria. Short taxi-boat rides from Naoussa harbor reach both.

Open in Google Maps
Day 9 · Half day

Parikia & the Church of 100 Doors

The ferry-port capital has a whitewashed old town and Panagia Ekatontapiliani, one of Greece's oldest Byzantine churches, plus cheaper sunset drinks than Naoussa.

Day 10 · Nightlife

Naoussa harbor after dark

The "cocktail capital of the Aegean": waterfront candlelit tavernas, then drinks at Barbarossa Bar, Sommaripa Consolato, or the legendary Pirate Bar.

◉Eat & drink
  • Barbarossa world-famous Naoussa harbor spot; reserve ahead
  • Siparos & Taverna Glafkos seaside dining at Santa Maria & Agios Dimitrios
⚽World Cup watch

Quarterfinals 3 & 4 (Sat Jul 11) run late, roughly midnight and 4 AM Athens time. July 12 and 13 are rest days before the semifinals.

Days 11–13

Athens & Home

The finale: the Acropolis and Parthenon, the colorful lanes of the Plaka, a last sunset over the city from Mount Lycabettus, then the daytime flight home.

The mainland

Athens

Jul 14 – Jul 16Zeus Dolce by Wyndham
Open in Google Maps
Day 11–12 · Sights

Acropolis, Parthenon & the Plaka

The monumental heart of Athens: go early to beat the heat and crowds, tour the Acropolis Museum below, then wander the cobbled Plaka lanes of markets, jewelers, and family tavernas.

Plan your visit
Day 12 · Sunset

Mount Lycabettus

Funicular or taxi to the summit for the city's best panoramic view and a final dinner over Athens.

◉Eat & drink
  • Psaras Taverna the Old Tavern of Psarras, serving since 1898
  • Scholarchio traditional taverna off the tourist path
⚽World Cup watch

You are here for the semifinals: Semifinal 1 (Tue Jul 14, Dallas) around 10 PM Athens time, Semifinal 2 (Wed Jul 15, Atlanta) the next night. Find a taverna with a TV.

Day 13 · 10:25 AM

Homeward on United UA 125

Early breakfast, check out of Zeus Dolce, and head to Athens Airport for the daytime crossing back to Newark, landing 2:05 PM local. You will be home just ahead of the July 19 Final, having caught both semifinals on the ground.

Manage booking
Before you go

Trip Reference

Packing Checklist

  • Passports & printed confirmations
  • EU plug adapter (Type E/F)
  • Comfortable walking shoes
  • Modest layer for churches
  • Swimsuits & reef-safe sunscreen
  • Light rain layer for Paris
  • Portable charger & eSIM
  • Kids' entertainment for transit
  • Reusable water bottles
  • Copy of travel insurance

Handy Phrases

Hello / Thank you (FR)Bonjour / Merci
The check, please (FR)L'addition, s'il vous plaît
Hello / Thank you (GR)Yassas / Efharistó
How much? (GR)Póso kani?
Cheers! (GR)Yamas!

Handy Apps

Get your travel apps ready before you go — Google Maps for navigation, airline apps for boarding passes, and a currency converter for euros.

bon voyage & kaló taxídi
Matusov & O'Brien · France & Greece · July 2026
This itinerary was assembled from hotel confirmations, flight records, and first-hand research. Times, routes, and availability change — always confirm with your provider before traveling.
